title: GraphAlg Playground: An Online Platform for Learning and Experimenting with the GraphAlg Language
abstract: The GraphAlg language for graph algorithms enables native support for user-defined graph analytics workloads in databases. In this demonstration, we present a web-based playground for writing and executing GraphAlg programs in the web browser, including an interactive tutorial explaining its key concepts. The playground runs inside the user's web browser without any installation, and is freely available under a permissive license as a reusable library. We present two demonstration scenarios of the publicly available playground website, showing how new users can learn to program in GraphAlg using the tutorial, while expert users can use the playground to prototype and validate their algorithms.
